heading: Behavior
body:
For `text` queries that use `synonyms` without `matchCriteria`,
MongoDB Search only matches documents which contain an exact match of the query terms
(with or without the expanded synonyms).

This behavior might change in the future and your query results might
also change. To avoid service disruptions, always use `matchCriteria`
when using `synonyms`. Or, use synonyms with the [Phrase](phrase-ref)
operator.

For example, suppose equivalent synonym mappings for `nyc` and `new
york city`. A search for `i like nyc` returns the following:

| Operator Specification | Default Behavior                           | "matchCriteria": "any"                      | "matchCriteria": "all"                        |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| text without synonyms  | Matches i OR like OR nyc                   | Matches i OR like OR nyc                    | Matches i AND like AND nyc                    |
| text with synonyms     | Matches i like nyc OR i like new york city | Matches i OR like OR (nyc OR new york city) | Matches i AND like AND (nyc OR new york city) |

The amount of time that MongoDB Search takes to execute queries that use
[Synonym](synonyms-ref) depends on the number and size of
documents in the synonym source collection. A query that uses a synonym
mapping that is based on very few synonym documents might be faster than
a query that uses a synonym mapping that is based on many synonym
documents.
